title: Calculating Monthly Totals with SUM
description: Learn to use the SUM function to calculate totals for monthly reports, budgets, and expense tracking.
objectives: Use SUMIF to total specific categories
Understand formula basics and cell references
content:
Formulas Make Excel Powerful

Excel formulas turn a static list of numbers into a dynamic tool. When you change a value, the total updates automatically. The SUM function is the most common formula in the workplace. It is used in budgets, payroll, sales reports, and inventory sheets every day.

Using SUM

Type =SUM(B2:B10) in a cell to add all numbers from B2 to B10. You can also click the AutoSum button and Excel will suggest the range. If you add a new row inside the range, Excel automatically includes it. If you add a row outside, you need to update the formula.

SUMIF for Category Totals

What if you want to see only the total for 'Travel' expenses, not all expenses? Use SUMIF. The formula looks like this: =SUMIF(C2:C50, 'Travel', D2:D50). This means: look in column C for the word 'Travel', and add the matching values from column D. This is how you build category reports in seconds.

Cell References

There are two types of cell references. Relative references (like B2) change when you copy the formula to a new row. Absolute references (like $B$2) stay the same. For a budget template where you always want to reference a tax rate in cell B2, use $B$2 so it does not shift when copied down.

downloads.title: SUM Formula Cheat Sheet
downloads.description: Quick reference for SUM and SUMIF

exercise: In an Excel spreadsheet with expenses in column D and categories in column C, write three SUMIF formulas to calculate totals for 'Travel', 'Meals', and 'Office Supplies' separately. Add a SUM formula for the grand total. Test by changing one value and watching the totals update.

quiz.question: What does the formula =SUM(B2:B10) do?
options: It adds all numbers from B2 to B10
quiz.explanation: SUM adds all the numbers in the specified range and displays the total.

quiz.question: Which function lets you add only rows that match a specific category?
quiz.explanation: SUMIF adds values only when a condition is met, such as a specific category name.

quiz.question: What is the difference between B2 and $B$2 in a formula?
options: B2 is relative and changes when copied; $B$2 is absolute and stays fixed
quiz.explanation: Relative references shift when copied. Absolute references (with $ signs) stay locked on the same cell.
